SLA Metric: 181 hours productivity loss since 5/14/2026; Network disruptions in DEV/QA

Risk Description: Because of recurring environment downtimes totaling 181 hours of team productivity loss since May 14, 2026, documented across WSR (JIRA-1009), DSR (Sprint 2), and QBR reports, combined with persistent network issues causing Enterprise services disruptions in DEV/QA environments and pending AKS cluster upgrades to 1.33, the root cause is inadequate infrastructure stability and delayed cloud infrastructure modernization. This systemic infrastructure fragility is causing development, testing, and integration activities to be severely disrupted across all PODs (Alpha, Cypher, Nexus, Apex), which propagates to compressed development cycles, reduced code quality due to interrupted workflows, extended testing timelines, and cascading schedule delays across Release 1 milestones. The business impact includes potential SLA breaches, increased project costs from extended timelines and resource inefficiency (current 70.4% utilization impacted), team morale degradation, delayed Release 1 go-live beyond mid-July/August 2026 target, and risk of customer dissatisfaction due to missed delivery commitments.
Tech Stack: Cloud Infrastructure, AWS, Azure, AKS, DevOps, CI/CD, Development Environment
Potential Impact: Continued productivity loss exceeding 181+ hours; cascading schedule delays across all releases; reduced code quality; team morale degradation; increased project costs; potential SLA breaches; delayed Release 1 go-live; customer escalations; contract penalty risk.

SLA Metric: Defect Density: 22.19 bugs/KLOC vs target 2 bugs/KLOC; Defect Leakage: ~4.8% (approaching 5% threshold); 116 QA defects across sprints 15-20
Risk Title: Critical Quality Crisis with Defect Density and Leakage Threatening Production Readiness
Risk Description: Because of actual defect density of 22.19 bugs per KLOC exceeding the target of 2 bugs per KLOC by over 11 times (documented in MBR/SOW and JIRA-1024), combined with 116 QA defects identified across sprints 15-20 and a defect leakage rate of approximately 4.8% approaching the critical 5% threshold (documented in WSR, DSR Sprint 2, and QBR), and QA cost overruns of INR 0.28L (6.7% over budget) driven by repeated regression and retesting cycles with QA resource utilization at 22% (4% above plan) as documented in Finance & Metrics Report, the root cause is systemic code quality issues stemming from inadequate root cause analysis, insufficient unit test coverage (only 70% achieved with 30% code untested), compressed development cycles due to approval delays, and architectural misalignments from late HLD approvals. This critical quality degradation is causing escaped defects to reach UAT and production environments, extensive rework consuming development capacity, prolonged testing cycles, and budget pressure in QA activities. The business impact includes customer-reported production defects, UAT delays and potential UAT failure, production incidents impacting end users, SLA breaches, increased support and maintenance costs, reputational damage in the finance sector where quality is paramount, potential contract penalties, and erosion of the favorable budget variance buffer (currently INR 0.58L total) threatening the 126% ROI projection.
Tech Stack: QA Testing, QA Automation Tools, JIRA, Test Automation, Testing Frameworks, .NET 8, SonarQube
Potential Impact: Customer-reported production defects; UAT failure risk; production incidents; extensive rework; SLA breaches; increased support costs; reputational damage; contract penalties; budget overrun; ROI degradation from 126%; customer trust erosion; potential rollback scenarios.
